channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Suspected US strikes pummel Houthi-controlled areas of Yemen
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes pummeled sites across Yemen controlled by the Houthi rebels early Friday, including neighborhoods in the capital, Sanaa.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US defense chief says stronger deterrence needed against China in disputed sea
item.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said Friday that the Trump administration would boost military ties with the Philippines to strengthen deterrence against “threats from the communist Chinese” and ensure freedom of navigation in the disputed South China Sea.
item.dc:creator: Jim Gomez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trial of former President Sarkozy sheds light on France’s back-channel talks with Libya’s Gadhafi
item.description: The trial of former French President Nicolas Sarkozy over the alleged illegal financing of his 2007 presidential campaign is shedding light on France’s back-channel talks with the government of then-Libyan leader Moammar Gadhafi.
item.dc:creator: Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Rain and cooler weather help South Korean fire crews battle devastating wildfires
item.description: Rain and cooler temperatures are helping South Korean fire crews as they battle the country’s worst-ever wildfires as the governor of the hardest-hit region called for overhauling response strategies to respond to the climate crisis that he says worsened the disaster.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim And Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Social media trends are driving Indonesia's patchouli oil industry. The cost? Deforestation
item.description: Demand for the patchouli plant’s oils has skyrocketed in recent years, and so too has the number of farmers in the region who now grow and process it.
item.dc:creator: Edna Tarigan, Dita Alangkara And Fadlan Syam, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Putin says US push for Greenland rooted in history, vows to uphold Russian interest in the Arctic
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia's President Vladimir Putin said Thursday that President Donald Trump's push for control over Greenland wasn't surprising given longtime U.S. interest in the mineral-rich territory.
item.dc:creator: VLADIMIR ISACHENKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: King Charles admitted to hospital over side effects from cancer treatment
item.description: The United Kingdom’s King Charles III was admitted to the hospital Thursday due to unexpected side effects from cancer treatments.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Greenland lawmakers form a new government on the eve of Vance visit
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Greenlandic lawmakers on Thursday agreed to form a new government, banding together to resist U.S. President Donald Trump’s efforts to annex the Arctic island, local media reported.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A stabbing attack in Amsterdam wounds five people, including four foreigners
item.description: AMSTERDAM (AP) — A knife-wielding assailant in Amsterdam seriously wounded five people — including two from the United States, one from Belgium and one from Poland — in a stabbing attack Thursday on a busy shopping street, Dutch police said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-President Martinelli gets Panama's permission to take asylum in Nicaragua
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Ex-Panama President Ricardo Martinelli will be allowed to leave the Nicaraguan embassy where he’s been holed up for more than a year to travel to Nicaragua for humanitarian reasons, Panama Foreign Affairs Minister Javier Martínez-Acha Vásquez said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US-Canada airline bookings plummet 70 percent: Report
item.description: Airline bookings between the U.S. and Canada are down by 70 percent compared to the same time last year, according to the aviation analytics company OAG.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Police use force to break up protests at a university in Turkey's capital
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Police used pepper spray, plastic pellets and water cannon against protesters in Turkey’s capital early Thursday, the latest clash in the country’s biggest anti-government protests in over a decade.
item.dc:creator: ROBERT BADENDIECK and ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New 2-clawed dinosaur discovered in Mongolia
item.description: Although the new species has large front claws, it is likely a herbivore who used them to grab leaves.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Danish PM says Greenlanders ‘have not been cowed’ ahead of Vance visit
item.description: Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen praised Greenlanders and said they “have not been cowed” ahead of a Friday visit by Vice President Vance and other Trump administration officials to Greenland. Greenland is an autonomous territory that is part of the Kingdom of Denmark.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Rubio defends detention of Tufts graduate student Rumeysa Ozturk
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio defended the detention of a foreign Ph.D. student at Tufts University on Tuesday. Rumeysa Ozturk, a Turkish national, was seen in a video arrested by plain-clothes federal officers and taken away in an unmarked van.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: South Sudan opposition says peace deal has collapsed after arrest of its leader
item.description: JUBA, South Sudan (AP) — South Sudan’s main opposition party said Thursday a peace agreement that ended a five-year civil war has collapsed, following the arrest of its leader Riek Machar a day earlier.
item.dc:creator: DENG MACHOL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Danish TV reporter: Americans knocked on doors in Greenland's capital; couldn't find hosts
item.description: A Danish television reporter said American officials knocked on doors in Greenland’s capital, Nuuk, seeking local hosts for second lady Usha Vance's planned visit, but they largely got a cold shoulder.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: US Homeland Security Secretary Noem visits Colombia to discuss immigration and crime
item.description: U.S. Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem has visited Colombia as part of a tour of three Latin American nations to discuss deportation, immigration and crime.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel's parliament approves a key part of Netanyahu's divisive judicial overhaul
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel’s parliament on Thursday passed a key part of Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s plan to overhaul the judiciary, angering critics who view it as a power grab by his far-right government.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Macron says a proposed European force for Ukraine could 'respond' if attacked by Russia
item.description: PARIS (AP) — French President Emmanuel Macron said Wednesday that a proposed European armed force for possible deployment in Ukraine in tandem with an eventual peace deal could “respond” to a Russian attack if Moscow launched one.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France's foreign minister is in China to discuss Ukraine and trade issues
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot began a two-day visit to China on Thursday and held talks with his Chinese counterpart on Ukraine and longstanding trade disputes between China and Europe.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes kill a family of 6 and a Hamas spokesman in Gaza
item.description: Separate Israeli strikes have killed a family of six and a Hamas spokesman in the northern Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Crowds flock to newest Catholic saint in Assisi
item.description: ASSISI, Italy (AP) — Pilgrims have been pouring into this medieval hilltop town to venerate not only two of the Catholic Church’s most celebrated saints, Francis and Clare, but its newest — Carlo Acutis, the first millennial saint, who will be canonized on April 27. “St. Francis, St.
item.dc:creator: GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel orders more evacuations and Palestinians stage rare anti-Hamas protests
item.description: The Israeli military on Wednesday ordered the evacuation of parts of Gaza City as it steps up its renewed offensive against Hamas after breaking the ceasefire last week.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Missing US soldiers in Lithuania killed: NATO chief
item.description: Four U.S. soldiers who went missing while training in Lithuania have died, NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte said Wednesday. Speaking to reporters during a trip to Warsaw, Rutte said he had received word of the deaths of the four American troops.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: The Atlantic releases full Signal text chain
item.description: The Atlantic editor-in-chief on Wednesday released the full text message chain between Trump administration officials.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: His brother’s keeper: Ilay David warns his brother, Hamas hostage Evyatar, is running out of time
item.description: Evyatar David's brother, Ilay, is fighting just to play music together again. For almost a year and a half, Evyatar has been held hostage in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gabbard, Ratcliffe, Patel criticized over Signal chat at hearing
item.description: Though the hearing is about the 2025 Annual Worldwide Threats Assessment, officials were questioned about a Signal group chat leak.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance joining his wife for Greenland trip on Friday
item.description: The delegation is set to tour the northernmost security installation for the U.S. military, the Space Force's Pituffik Space Base.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: Europe tight-lipped following Hegseth, Vance 'loathing' text exchange
item.description: European leaders remain tight-lipped after "loathing" comments were issued by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Vice President JD Vance in leaked text exchange.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two military jets collide in midair stunt, crash to ground in fiery explosion
item.description: Two Alpha jets collided midair during a training exercise in France before crashing to the ground and exploding in a massive fireball, dramatic video shows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anti-Hamas protests break out in Gaza Strip over demands to end war
item.description: Palestinians protested in northern Gaza, where they reportedly demanded an end to the war in anti-Hamas protests. The last major protest against the terror group occurred in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cruise passengers issued warning while passing through pirate hot spot
item.description: Passengers aboard the "Queen Anne" ship were issued an alarming warning while passing through a pirate hot spot.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US holds separate talks with Russians after meeting Ukrainians to discuss a potential ceasefire
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — U.S. negotiators worked on a proposed partial ceasefire in the 3-year-old war in Ukraine on Monday, meeting representatives from Russia one day after holding separate talks with a team from Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Gaza's Health Ministry says Israel has struck the largest hospital in the territory's south
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel’s military struck the largest hospital in southern Gaza on Sunday night, killing two people, wounding others and causing a large fire, the territory's Health Ministry said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UK issues travel warning for US
item.description: The United Kingdom updated its advice for travel to the United States on Thursday, warning of harsh consequences for British passport holders who violate U.S. immigration laws.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9
